stem:
Many desert plants, such as the saguaro cactus, have shallow but very wide-spreading root systems. When rare rainstorms occur in the desert, water soaks only the top few inches of soil before evaporating in the heat. Plants with deep taproots cannot reach this thin layer of moisture in time, while plants with wide shallow roots can absorb water across a large area quickly. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: This suggests that the saguaro's shallow, wide root system is an adaptation that allows it to efficiently collect scarce surface water before it evaporates.
Therefore, saguaro cacti must grow only in places where it never rains.
This proves that all desert plants share the exact same root structure.
Scientists concluded that cacti do not actually need water to survive.

explanation: The passage links shallow roots to the ability to capture surface water quickly before it evaporates, directly supporting the adaptation conclusion.

stem:
Honeybees perform a "waggle dance" to communicate the location of food sources to other members of their hive. The angle of the dance indicates the direction of the food relative to the sun, and the duration of the waggle portion corresponds to the distance. Bees watching the dance are then able to fly directly to food sources they have never visited before. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Researchers concluded that the waggle dance is performed purely for entertainment.
Therefore, honeybees are the only insects capable of any form of communication.
This means that honeybees cannot actually find food on their own.
This indicates that honeybees can transmit precise spatial information to one another through a symbolic movement-based communication system.

explanation: The text describes the dance encoding direction and distance so other bees can locate unfamiliar food, which is the definition of symbolic spatial communication.

stem:
A middle school tested two versions of its lunch menu over a semester. In the first version, vegetables were placed at the end of the serving line, after the pizza and sandwiches. In the second version, vegetables were moved to the front of the line. Cafeteria staff recorded that vegetable servings taken per student nearly doubled during the weeks vegetables were placed first. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Therefore, students must dislike pizza and sandwiches.
This suggests that the order in which food is presented can influence the choices students make in the lunch line.
This proves that students at this school will always choose vegetables over any other food.
Researchers concluded that cafeteria staff are responsible for students' food preferences.

explanation: Only the placement order changed, and vegetable servings nearly doubled, which directly supports the conclusion that presentation order affects choice.

stem:
Olive trees are famous for their extraordinary lifespans; some individual trees in the Mediterranean are estimated to be over two thousand years old. Researchers have found that these ancient trees survive droughts, fires, and freezes that kill younger trees of the same species. Unlike younger olives, which rely on a single trunk, ancient trees typically have multiple trunks and sprawling root systems that regenerate from below the soil. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: This implies that olive trees cannot survive in any climate other than the Mediterranean.
Therefore, all trees older than a thousand years must be olive trees.
Scientists concluded that fire is actually beneficial to olive trees.
This suggests that the multi-trunked structure of ancient olive trees contributes to their ability to survive environmental threats that kill younger trees.

explanation: The passage contrasts young single-trunked olives (which die from threats) with ancient multi-trunked ones (which survive them), directly linking structure to resilience.

stem:
When the first public libraries opened in major U.S. cities in the late 1800s, most were limited to adult readers. A librarian named Anne Carroll Moore argued that children should have their own library spaces with small chairs, low shelves, and books chosen for their interests. After she opened the first children's reading room in New York City in 1911, library attendance by children in the city rose sharply, and other cities soon created similar rooms of their own. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Therefore, adults stopped visiting libraries after 1911.
This means Anne Carroll Moore invented the concept of reading.
This suggests that designing library spaces specifically for children can make libraries more welcoming and accessible to young readers.
Researchers concluded that children cannot read without adult supervision.

explanation: The passage shows that dedicated child-friendly spaces led directly to higher attendance and widespread imitation, supporting the design-accessibility conclusion.

stem:
Sea otters spend much of their day grooming their fur. Unlike whales or seals, sea otters lack a thick layer of blubber to insulate them from the cold ocean. Instead, their extremely dense fur traps a layer of air next to the skin, which keeps them warm. If the fur becomes dirty or matted, this air layer collapses, and the otter loses body heat rapidly. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Scientists concluded that otters groom each other to show affection only.
Therefore, sea otters must never enter the water.
This implies that sea otters groom themselves primarily to maintain the insulating properties of their fur.
This proves that all marine mammals lack blubber.

explanation: The passage explains that clean fur traps insulating air while matted fur loses heat, directly linking grooming to insulation.

stem:
A small town experimented with replacing traditional streetlights with motion-sensing lights along a walking path beside the river. The new lights stayed dim when no one was present and brightened when they detected pedestrians or cyclists. After one year, the town reported that energy use along the path dropped by about 60 percent compared to the previous lighting system. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Therefore, no one walks along the river path anymore.
Scientists concluded that motion sensors always fail in cold weather.
This proves that traditional streetlights are no longer manufactured anywhere.
This suggests that motion-sensing lights can significantly reduce the energy used to illuminate public paths.

explanation: The town measured a 60% drop in energy use after the switch, which directly supports the conclusion that motion lights cut energy consumption.

stem:
Crows are among the few non-human animals known to use tools. In one experiment, a New Caledonian crow was presented with a piece of food at the bottom of a narrow tube too deep to reach by beak. The crow was given a straight wire and, after several attempts to reach the food, bent the wire into a hook and used it to pull the food out of the tube. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: This indicates that the crow was able to modify an object in order to solve a problem it had not encountered before.
Researchers concluded that the crow had been taught to bend wire by humans.
Therefore, crows are smarter than all other birds on Earth.
This proves that crows prefer wire to their natural diet.

explanation: The crow bent the wire on its own after failing with a straight one, which directly supports the conclusion that it modified a tool to solve a novel problem.

stem:
Archaeologists studying a 3,000-year-old settlement in Peru found pottery fragments decorated with images of fish, along with stone tools shaped for cutting and scraping. The site itself, however, is located nearly 30 miles inland from the nearest coastline, and no fish bones have been recovered from the surrounding soil. The pottery designs closely resemble those produced by coastal communities of the same period. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Therefore, the site must once have been located directly on the coast.
This suggests that the inland community had some form of contact or exchange with coastal groups, even though they did not eat fish themselves.
This proves that the stone tools were used only for making pottery.
Researchers concluded that the pottery was imported from Europe.

explanation: Coast-style pottery without fish bones points to cultural exchange rather than local fish consumption, and the other choices go beyond what the evidence supports.

stem:
In a study on sleep and memory, college students were taught a list of new vocabulary words. Half of the students were allowed to sleep for eight hours that night, while the other half were kept awake. The next morning, both groups were tested on the words. Students who slept remembered about 30 percent more words than those who stayed awake, even though both groups had spent the same amount of time studying the list. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: This implies that sleep plays a role in consolidating newly learned information into memory.
Therefore, students who sleep eight hours every night will never forget anything.
This proves that study time has no effect on memory at all.
Researchers concluded that staying awake improves recall of vocabulary.

explanation: With study time held constant, the only difference between groups was sleep, and the sleep group remembered more, supporting the consolidation conclusion.

stem:
Writer Zora Neale Hurston spent years in the 1930s traveling through Florida and the Caribbean, recording folktales, songs, and customs in the communities she visited. At the time, few academic folklorists considered these oral traditions worth preserving in writing, and many of the stories Hurston collected had never appeared in any book before. Today, her field notes are used as primary sources by historians and literary scholars. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: This proves that Hurston was the first person to study folklore anywhere in the world.
Therefore, modern historians no longer need any other primary sources.
Scientists concluded that oral traditions are always more reliable than written ones.
This suggests that Hurston's work preserved cultural material that might otherwise have been lost to scholarly record.

explanation: Hurston recorded material that had never been written down and that scholars now rely on, which directly supports the conclusion that her work preserved otherwise-lost records.

stem:
Some species of frogs in Central America have evolved bright warning colors on their skin, such as vivid reds, yellows, and blues. These same species also secrete powerful toxins through their skin, making them dangerous for predators to eat. Closely related frog species that lack such toxins tend to have dull, mottled brown coloration that blends into leaf litter on the forest floor. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Therefore, any brightly colored animal is guaranteed to be poisonous.
This suggests that bright coloration in these frogs functions as a signal that warns predators of their toxicity.
This means that predators in Central America are colorblind.
Scientists concluded that camouflage is the only effective defense in the forest.

explanation: Toxic frogs are bright while non-toxic relatives are camouflaged, which directly supports the conclusion that color signals toxicity.

stem:
A company that produces online courses tested two different layouts for its video lessons. In the first layout, each video was followed by a short quiz before the next video could be played. In the second layout, all the videos played in a row and the quiz came only at the end of the unit. Students in the first group completed more of the course overall and scored higher on the final unit exam than those in the second group. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Therefore, online courses should never include videos.
This proves that students will only watch videos if they are forced to take a quiz.
This indicates that embedding short quizzes between videos may support both course completion and learning outcomes.
Researchers concluded that the final exam had no relationship to the videos.

explanation: The quiz-between-videos group had higher completion and higher final scores, directly supporting the conclusion that embedded quizzes help both outcomes.

stem:
Glassmakers in medieval Venice developed such refined techniques for producing clear, colorful glass that Venetian glass became a highly sought-after luxury across Europe. To protect these techniques, the Venetian government confined all glassmakers to the island of Murano and passed laws forbidding them from leaving the republic. Glassmakers who attempted to take their skills abroad faced heavy penalties, and their families in Venice could be imprisoned. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Therefore, no one outside of Venice ever learned to make glass.
This suggests that the Venetian government viewed the glassmakers' specialized knowledge as a valuable economic asset worth protecting by law.
This proves that glassmakers in Venice were paid more than any other workers in Europe.
Researchers concluded that Venetian glass was of lower quality than other European glass.

explanation: Confining glassmakers and punishing defectors is consistent only with treating their know-how as a protected economic asset.

stem:
Fossils of a small dinosaur called Microraptor show that it had feathers not only on its arms but also on its legs, giving it four feathered limbs. Detailed analysis of the fossilized feathers reveals that they were long, stiff, and asymmetrical, similar in shape to the flight feathers of modern birds rather than the soft down used only for insulation. No purely ground-running dinosaur has been found with feathers shaped this way. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: This implies that Microraptor's feathers were adapted for gliding or flight rather than solely for keeping the animal warm.
Therefore, Microraptor must be a direct ancestor of all modern birds.
This proves that all dinosaurs had feathers on their legs.
Scientists concluded that Microraptor was actually a modern bird that fell into ancient rock.

explanation: Stiff, asymmetrical flight-style feathers, absent in ground-runners, point to an aerial function rather than insulation.

stem:
Between 1850 and 1900, cities across the United States rapidly expanded their networks of horse-drawn streetcars. These streetcars allowed workers to live farther from the factories and shops where they worked, and neighborhoods of modest homes began to grow along the streetcar lines. Before this period, most workers in American cities had lived within walking distance of their jobs. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Therefore, American cities stopped growing after 1900.
This suggests that the spread of streetcar lines contributed to a change in where working-class Americans could choose to live.
This proves that walking to work was no longer possible anywhere in the United States.
Researchers concluded that streetcars were more popular than automobiles in every era.

explanation: Streetcar lines enabled workers to live farther from jobs and neighborhoods grew along those lines, directly supporting the conclusion about residential change.

stem:
The poet Emily Dickinson published fewer than a dozen poems during her lifetime, even though she wrote nearly eighteen hundred. The few poems that did appear in newspapers and anthologies were heavily edited by publishers who changed her punctuation, regularized her irregular capitalization, and softened her unconventional metaphors. Dickinson complained in letters to friends about these alterations. After her death, her sister found the remaining poems in a locked chest and published them largely as Dickinson had written them. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Researchers concluded that Dickinson stopped writing poetry once her work was edited.
Therefore, Dickinson was unwilling to share her poetry with anyone she knew.
This proves that the edited versions of the poems were of higher literary quality than the originals.
This indicates that editorial changes to Dickinson's published work during her lifetime may not have reflected the form she intended for her poetry.

explanation: Publishers altered her work and Dickinson complained about those alterations, which directly supports the conclusion that the published versions did not match her intent.

stem:
Biologists studying a remote lake introduced a tagged population of a small fish species to study its movements. Within two years, the tagged fish had spread throughout the lake, but the overall size of the fish population had not grown. At the same time, the number of a second fish species native to the lake fell sharply in the same period, while water quality and temperature remained unchanged. No other new species were introduced, and no fishing was permitted during the study. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: This implies that the introduced fish and the native species may be competing for the same limited resources in the lake.
Therefore, the tagged fish must have left the lake entirely.
This proves that water quality is the only factor affecting fish populations.
Scientists concluded that tagging fish causes other species to disappear.

explanation: With water, temperature, and fishing ruled out, a stable introduced population alongside a falling native population most cleanly fits resource competition.

stem:
In early twentieth-century jazz, most musicians learned to play by ear, copying phrases from live performances and records rather than from written scores. Because each musician absorbed the music slightly differently, the same song could sound quite different depending on who played it. When jazz began to be taught in music schools later in the century, students were often given printed sheet music and encouraged to play the notes exactly as written. Critics of this shift argued that something essential to the style was being lost. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Researchers concluded that music schools produced the first jazz musicians.
Therefore, sheet music is never used by professional jazz musicians today.
This proves that jazz musicians in the early twentieth century could not read music at all.
This suggests that the critics believed the individual variation produced by ear-based learning was itself an important feature of the jazz tradition.

explanation: The critics objected to the shift away from ear-based learning that produced variation, so their view must value that variation itself as essential to jazz.

stem:
Medieval European maps often depicted Jerusalem at the exact center of the world, with Europe, Asia, and Africa arranged around it in a roughly circular pattern. These maps frequently included mythical creatures and biblical scenes along their edges but gave far less attention to accurate coastlines or distances. Medieval scholars were aware of more geographically precise maps produced by earlier Greek and Roman cartographers, and some even studied them. ______

Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: Therefore, medieval Europeans had no knowledge of any land beyond Jerusalem.
This implies that the mapmakers were primarily interested in conveying religious and symbolic meaning rather than producing geographically accurate images of the world.
This proves that Greek and Roman maps were never seen by medieval scholars.
Scientists concluded that mythical creatures actually lived at the edges of the medieval world.

explanation: Medieval mapmakers had access to accurate models but chose Jerusalem-centered, myth-laden layouts, pointing to symbolic over geographic intent.
